description_long: This object is a historical artillery cannon mounted on a wooden carriage with four solid wheels. The cannon barrel is long, cylindrical, and made of dark greenish metal, featuring a slightly flared muzzle and decorative bands along its length. The carriage is constructed from textured wood, reinforced with metal bolts and brackets, and includes two pairs of spoked wheels for mobility. Chains and small metal fixtures are attached to the carriage, likely for securing or maneuvering the cannon. The overall design is reminiscent of cannons used in the 18th or 19th centuries, with a robust and utilitarian appearance. The surface of the cannon is slightly glossy, while the carriage has a rougher, matte finish.
description: A historical artillery cannon with a long greenish metal barrel mounted on a wooden carriage with four wheels and metal reinforcements. The cannon features decorative bands, chains, and a robust, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular muzzle of the cannon barrel and the symmetrical arrangement of the wooden carriage and wheels.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the cannon barrel, the wooden carriage, and the details of the wheels and metal reinforcements.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the breech of the cannon, the attachment points for chains, and the back ends of the carriage and wheels.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the length of the barrel, carriage, and wheel details from the other side.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One fixed cannon barrel mounted centrally on the carriage; no additional weapon mounts visible.
attribute_armor_plating: None visible; the object consists of a metal barrel and wooden carriage with no additional armor plating.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: Solid dark green color on the barrel with no visible camouflage pattern.
